close_reason: The linux-native feature is needed because secretx-keyring IS the Linux-specific backend. Cross-platform users should use secretx-desktop. The crate correctly gates its inventory registration with #[cfg(target_os = linux)]. The Cargo.toml dependency isn't target-gated because the crate itself is Linux-only.
description: secretx-keyring/Cargo.toml declares keyring = { version = '3', features = ['linux-native', 'crypto-rust'] } as an unconditional dependency. The linux-native feature pulls in Linux-specific APIs (kernel keyring syscalls via keyutils-sys) that do not compile on macOS or Windows. The inventory submissions are correctly guarded with #[cfg(target_os = 'linux')] in lib.rs, but the dependency itself will fail to compile on non-Linux hosts. secretx-desktop uses the same keyring crate but with apple-native + windows-native + sync-secret-service features, showing cross-platform feature control is understood. Fix: move the keyring dependency in secretx-keyring to [target.'cfg(target_os = "linux")'.dependencies] so cross-platform workspace builds do not fail to compile this crate on macOS/Windows.
title: secretx-keyring dependency not target-gated for Linux-only keyring features
